marketTitle: Will Tommy McMillen win the McMillen vs Montes professional MMA fight scheduled for Jul 18, 2026?
source: authenticated Kalshi UFC Quick Order ticket, $500, NOT submitted
note: THE SIZE-CONFOUND TEST. Same market and same price (0.59) as the $100 example, 5x the size. The first three tickets were all $100, so contracts was not an independent observation — C = (100 - fee)/price made size a mechanical function of price, and rivals of form k*gross^x with x in 0.8-1.5 fitted all three. This point holds price fixed and varies size alone: 164.76 -> 823.81 contracts is a 5.0001x size ratio producing a 5.0000x fee ratio, refuting every exponent but 1.0 (x=0.8 predicts 10.11, x=1.5 predicts 31.19, actual 13.95). Linearity in contracts is measured here, not assumed.

marketTitle: Will Tommy McMillen win the McMillen vs Montes professional MMA fight scheduled for Jul 18, 2026?
source: authenticated Kalshi UFC Quick Order ticket, $50, NOT submitted
note: THE SMALL-SIZE TEST, and the smallest quantity verified. Same market and price (0.59) as the $100 and $500 tickets, so linearity in contracts is now measured across a 10x span at a fixed price: 82.37 -> 823.81 contracts (10.0013x) produces exact pre-ceil fees 1.394771 -> 13.949575 (10.0013x), identical to 6 decimal places. This ticket also observes the ceil premium directly rather than inferring it: ceil adds 0.375% of the fee at 82.37 contracts versus 0.003% at 823.81, confirming the discretization grows at small size. Practically decisive: at a $10k bankroll the 0.5% position cap proposes ~85 contracts, which previously sat BELOW the verified floor of 111.49 and now sits inside it. Note this ticket does NOT tighten the rate — a $1.40 fee has only 140 cents of resolution, so its own admissible rate interval is 502ppm wide against the 50ppm intersection from the larger tickets. Its value is size coverage, not rate precision.

marketTitle: Will Tommy McMillen win the McMillen vs Montes professional MMA fight scheduled for Jul 18, 2026?
source: authenticated Kalshi UFC Quick Order ticket, $2, NOT submitted
note: THE SMALL-ORDER FLOOR. Sets the verified size floor at 3.28 contracts and puts the $2 entertainment tier inside the envelope AT 0.59 ONLY — the floor is in CONTRACTS, so $2 buys just 2.22 contracts at 0.89 and falls back outside. Measures the ceil-dominated regime directly: ceil adds 8.03% of the fee here versus 0.003% at 823.81. Does NOT tighten the rate — a 6-cent fee admits rates from 0.063 to 0.076 (12603ppm) against the 50ppm intersection.

marketTitle: Will Tommy McMillen win the McMillen vs Montes professional MMA fight scheduled for Jul 18, 2026?
